title: Color Handling
text: Color Handling The objects that make up an application's user interface have color properties that determine foreground and background colors. These properties can be set in the Uniface IDE when defining each object, but they can also be set dynamically using ProcScript. Depending on the property, you can specify an object's color as a system color name, web color name, or RGB value (expressed as decimal or hexadecimal values). You can influence the color of an object in the following ways: Declaratively, by setting the object's Foreground Color and Background Color properties in the Properties Inspector. Procedurally, using ProcScript functions ( $windowproperties , $properties , and $fieldproperties ) to set and get a component, entity, or field's widget color properties. In deployment, using the application's .ini file to: Assign default colors to widgets, panels, and toolbars Define the Uniface index color combinations Note: Uniface follows Windows behavior in applying colors. Depending on the Windows version and the widget, the color may not always be applied. Note: Some objects have a legacy Color property which should be set to No when using Foreground Color and Background Color . It is useful for compatibility with older Uniface applications but is not recommended for new applications. You can enable endusers to choose colors using the Color Box and Color List widgets (enabling the user to enter or select a color from a list) and the colorbox ProcScript statement (which starts the Microsoft Windows Color dialog box for the user to select a color). Related Links Foreground Color (ForeColor) Color to use as the foreground (usually the text color). Background Color (BackColor) Color to use as the background of the object. Index Color Code that identifies a Uniface index color, which represents a combination of background and foreground colors. Applicable only to Form components. Deprecated. ucolorbox (ColorBox) The ucolorbox widget displays a color value, either a color name or RGB value, in a color box in a form component. Uniface provides a logical widget called ColorBox, which is mapped to ucolorbox. ucolorlist (ColorList) The (ucolorlist) widget displays a list of system or web colors for selection in a form component. Uniface provides a logical widget called ColorList, which is mapped to ucolorlist. colorbox Start the Microsoft Windows Color dialog box. $properties Return or set the current widget properties of a field. $fieldproperties Return or set the current widget properties of an instance of a field. $windowproperties Set or return the current window properties of a Form instance.
